questions.question_text: A married couple in Massachusetts takes title without specifying a form of ownership. The default form will be:
questions.explanation: Under MGL c.184 §7, a conveyance to two or more persons — including a married couple — creates a tenancy in common unless the deed expressly states otherwise. To obtain tenancy by the entirety and its survivorship and creditor protections, the deed must expressly say 'as tenants by the entirety.'

questions.question_text: Tenancy by the entirety in Massachusetts provides protection from:
distractors: Joint debts owed by both spouses
questions.explanation: Under MGL c.209 §1, property held as tenants by the entirety cannot be seized or executed on by a creditor of one spouse alone while it remains the principal residence of the nondebtor spouse (debts for family necessaries excepted). Joint debts and tax liens are not protected.

questions.question_text: A nominee trust (realty trust) is commonly used in Massachusetts for which purpose?
questions.correct_answer: Privacy and convenience in holding title
distractors: Eliminating the need for probate only
questions.explanation: Nominee trusts (realty trusts) are widely used in Massachusetts to hold title for privacy (the trust name appears on records, not the individual) and convenience in managing property.

questions.question_text: While married, one spouse who holds property in a tenancy by the entirety wants to sell the entire property without the other spouse's consent. This is:
distractors: Permitted if they own more than 50%
questions.explanation: One spouse acting alone cannot convey or encumber the entire estate held by the entirety. Under Coraccio v. Lowell Five (SJC 1993), a spouse may transfer only his or her own interest, and any such transfer remains subject to the other spouse's survivorship rights — so the whole property cannot be sold without both spouses' consent.

questions.question_text: A life estate holder in Massachusetts can do all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.correct_answer: Sell the property in fee simple
distractors: Occupy the property during their lifetime
Make reasonable improvements to the property
questions.explanation: A life estate grants lifetime use but not full ownership. The holder can occupy, rent, and improve the property, but cannot convey fee simple title because the remainder interest belongs to another party.

questions.question_text: Under the Massachusetts Condominium Act (Chapter 183A), a condominium is created by recording:
questions.correct_answer: A Master Deed and Declaration of Trust
questions.explanation: Condominiums are created by recording a Master Deed (describes units and common areas) and Declaration of Trust (establishes governance). These founding documents are filed at the Registry of Deeds.

questions.question_text: A buyer is purchasing a Massachusetts condominium unit. Which certificate, obtained from the organization of unit owners, allows the unit to pass to the buyer free of any lien for unpaid common expenses?
questions.explanation: Under MGL c.183A §6(d), the organization of unit owners must furnish a statement of unpaid common expenses within 10 business days of a written request; when recorded, it discharges the unit from the lien for amounts not listed. Unlike many states, Chapter 183A has no general resale-certificate or disclosure-package requirement.

questions.question_text: In a cooperative, residents own shares in a corporation and receive:
questions.correct_answer: A proprietary lease for their unit
distractors: Fee simple title to their unit
A Certificate of Title from Land Court
questions.explanation: Cooperative residents own shares in a corporation that owns the building and receive a proprietary lease granting them the right to occupy their specific unit.

questions.question_text: The Organization of Unit Owners in a Massachusetts condominium is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The Organization of Unit Owners (similar to an HOA) manages common areas, collects assessments, maintains insurance, and enforces rules. It does not approve sales, set taxes, or issue permits.

questions.question_text: A co-op board denies a prospective buyer based solely on their race. This action is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation of fair housing laws
distractors: Permitted for co-ops but not condos
Legal if the bylaws allow it
questions.explanation: While co-op boards can approve or deny purchasers, they must comply with fair housing laws. Denying a buyer based on race (or any other protected class) is illegal discrimination.

questions.question_text: Massachusetts's Torrens system for registered land is administered by:
questions.explanation: The Torrens system in Massachusetts is administered by the Land Court. Property registered with the Land Court has a Certificate of Title that serves as conclusive evidence of ownership.

questions.question_text: Registered land in Massachusetts provides what level of title protection compared to the traditional recording system?
questions.explanation: Registration provides stronger title protection than traditional recording. The Certificate of Title is conclusive evidence of ownership, whereas recorded deeds can still have title defects.

questions.question_text: Most land in Massachusetts uses which title system?
questions.explanation: While Massachusetts has a Torrens system, most land uses the traditional recording system through the county Registry of Deeds. Registered land is the exception, not the norm.

questions.question_text: A buyer purchases registered land. Transfers of this property must be recorded with:
questions.explanation: Transfers of registered land must be recorded with the Land Court registry, not the traditional county Registry of Deeds. The Land Court maintains the Certificate of Title system.

questions.question_text: A Certificate of Title issued by the Land Court serves as:
distractors: Evidence of ownership subject to challenge
questions.explanation: The Certificate of Title is conclusive evidence of ownership — it provides the highest level of certainty about who owns the property. This is the key advantage of the Torrens system.
